pageCaption.value: In 1998, Louis Liebenberg won a Rolex Award for his CyberTracker, a digital tool that enabled indigenous trackers to monetize their skills by recording their observations of wildlife on a handheld device to aid conservation.

WaPageTitle.value: Louis Liebenberg's CyberTracker Online - Perpetual Planet - Video

metadescription.value: Louis Liebenberg's CyberTracker Online allows indigenous trackers to monetize their ancient skills while safeguarding biodiversity. More on rolex.org.

text.value: In 1998, Louis Liebenberg won a Rolex Award for his CyberTracker, a digital tool that enabled indigenous trackers to monetize their skills by recording their observations of wildlife on a handheld device to aid conservation. Downloaded more than 600,000 times, CyberTracker is used in some 200 countries. Now Liebenberg is launching CyberTracker Online, an updated version of the tool for the internet age. The new features could have significant implications for conservation, education and the preservation of the ancient art of tracking.

pageCaption.value: Brazilian environmentalist José Márcio Ayres, who died in March 2003, devoted his life to the challenge of combining protection of the Amazon forest with improving the living conditions of its native people.

metadescription.value: Brazilian environmentalist José Márcio Ayres, who died in March 2003, devoted his life to the challenge of combining protection of the Amazon forest with improving the living conditions of its native people.

pageCaption.value: American Christine Keung is using her college education as a force for good by tackling rural pollution in northwest China, where her father was sent during the Cultural Revolution. Her ambition is to help authorities find long-term solutions to hazardous waste, and women – who bear the disproportionate cost of environmental degradation as men migrate to cities for work – are the key to her project.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards Video - A vanished forest reborn

pageCaption.value: Working with dedicated farming families, Laury Cullen Jr. is restoring Brazil’s Atlantic Forest while contributing to the local economy and helping to fight climate change.

metadescription.value: Laury Cullen Jr.’s Dream Map initiative has overseen the planting of four million trees in Brazil’s Atlantic Forest. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageCaption.value: National Geographic Explorer Dr. Paula Kahumbu has devoted her career to protecting elephants from environmental changes and poachers.

pageTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate - Inza Koné - Episode 1

pageCaption.value: As a 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate, Inza Koné is protecting one of Côte d’Ivoire’s last remaining primary rainforests, Tanoé-Ehy. The forest teems with life, including species found nowhere else on Earth.

WaPageTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate - Inza Koné - Episode 1

metadescription.value: Primatologist Inza Koné has devoted his life to advocating for the endemic primates of Côte d'Ivoire's Tanoé-Ehy forest. Watch the video on rolex.org.
